solutions.solution: In Bihar, the mid-day meal scheme won't work because there is no proper reason why Bihar won't take care of mid-day meals. Explanation: There is no reason why Bihar (identical conditions) won't take care that Bihar and Tamil Nadu will definitely false that the property exhibited by Tamil Nadu will not be exhibited in Bihar.

A person who goes on a diet control (weight reduction programme) for a long period, will also develop muscular inefficiency. Explanation: We have seen that children who suffer from malnutrition develop muscular inefficiency. Now, a person on diet control deliberately starves himself of food and hence he is also - in some way - similar to a malnourished man. But, this is not exactly a case of malnutrition; there are some differences. Thus, this group is similar but not exactly similar to the first group. Hence, it is probably true that he will also develop muscular inefficiency.

India has pursued the policy of non-alignment since Independence. Though a little inclined towards the former USSR, it has been able to establish a graceful position as a neutral nation. Consequently, it has been able to achieve the much-needed foreign aids without much difficulty. Inference: Sri Lanka would have a lot of difficulty in getting foreign aids. Explanation: India and Sri Lanka are both part of the Non-Aligned Movement and hence they can be said to be similar in this aspect. But they are also roughly similar because other conditions (that influence foreign aid) might be different. Now, India has been shown to get foreign aid without much hassles and it has been linked with its non-aligned status. Hence, it would be probably false that a similar (non-aligned) nation should have a lot of difficulty in getting aids. But it can't be declared definitely false because India and Sri Lanka can't be assumed to be exactly similar.

Schoolchildren in Tamil Nadu need no further nutrition. Explanation: We can safely assume that one meal in a day (mid-day meal) is not sufficient for nutrition. Hence, the given inference must be definitely false.

Schoolchildren in Tamil Nadu need no further nutrition. Explanation: We can safely assume that one meal in a day (mid-day meal) is not sufficient for nutrition. Hence, the given inference must be definitely false.

The accident that occurred was fatal. Explanation: The passage says that the man was run down by a car. We can assume that when a man is run down by a car, he is extremely likely to die. Hence, the inference is probably true. It is not definitely true because the man may have escaped death in spite of being run down. [There are always miraculous escapes from death.]

Some countries which have adopted privatisation are considering a change over to nationalisation now. Explanation: The passage says that privatisation has become the 'buzzword' today. Hence, it is unlikely that any country would be swimming against the tide and thinking of nationalisation. Hence, the inference is probably false. However, it is not definitely false because there are always exceptions. (A good example of the same could be Nepal, where - in today's world of falling Communism - the Communist Party still dominates.)
